member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.AlpacaOptions
member.summary: Alpaca Market Data configuration.

remarks.para: Authentication: Uses Trading API keys with WebSocket message authentication.

item.description: Use a secure vault service (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault)
Use .NET User Secrets for local development: dotnet user-secrets
Ensure appsettings.json with real credentials is in .gitignore

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Config.AlpacaOptions.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Alpaca Market Data configuration.

remarks.para: Authentication: Uses Trading API keys with WebSocket message authentication.

item.description: Use a secure vault service (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault)
Use .NET User Secrets for local development: dotnet user-secrets
Ensure appsettings.json with real credentials is in .gitignore

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.AppConfig
member.summary: Root configuration model loaded from appsettings.json.
member.param: Output directory root for storage sinks.
Whether JSONL sinks should gzip. Null means use base configuration/default.

Market data provider selector: - uses Interactive Brokers via IMarketDataClient/IBMarketDataClient. - uses Alpaca market data via WebSocket (trades; quotes optional in future). - uses StockSharp connectors (Rithmic, IQFeed, CQG, IB, etc.). - uses the NYSE market data feed. - uses the built-in synthetic historical/reference dataset for offline development.
Alpaca provider options (required if DataSource == DataSourceKind.Alpaca).
Interactive Brokers provider options (required if DataSource == DataSourceKind.IB).
Polygon provider options (required if DataSource == DataSourceKind.Polygon).
StockSharp connector configuration (required if DataSource == DataSourceKind.StockSharp).
Synthetic market-data provider configuration for offline/backtest development.
Storage configuration options (naming convention, partitioning, etc.).
Multiple data source configurations for real-time and historical data.
Relationship-aware provider connections, bindings, policies, presets, and certifications.
Unified provider registry configuration controlling attribute-based discovery.
Canonicalization configuration for condition codes and venue MICs.
Configuration for the F# validation pipeline stage.
When true, enables air-gapped offline-first mode: backfill requests are queued and deferred until connectivity is restored. Default is false.
Optional directory path for loading external data source plugins. When set, plugins are loaded and registered dynamically.
When true, activates exchange colocation profile: low-latency GC settings and network tuning. Default is false.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.ProviderRegistryConfig
member.summary: Configuration for the unified provider registry (Phase 1.2). Controls how streaming, backfill, and symbol search providers are discovered and registered.
member.param: When true, DataSourceAttribute-decorated types are discovered via reflection and automatically registered as streaming factories in the ProviderRegistry, replacing manual lambda registration. Default is false (manual registration).

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.ValidationPipelineConfig
member.summary: Configuration for the F# validation pipeline stage. When enabled, every incoming is validated against the F# Railway-Oriented validators before it is persisted. Events that fail validation are written to the dead-letter sink instead of primary storage.
member.param: When , the F# validation stage is activated. Defaults to to preserve backward-compatible behaviour.

When , stricter real-time configuration is applied: timestamp max-age drops from 5 minutes to 5 seconds, and sequence numbers are checked for continuity. Disable for historical backfill or replay scenarios.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Config.ValidationPipelineConfig.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Configuration for the F# validation pipeline stage. When enabled, every incoming is validated against the F# Railway-Oriented validators before it is persisted. Events that fail validation are written to the dead-letter sink instead of primary storage.
member.param: When , the F# validation stage is activated. Defaults to to preserve backward-compatible behaviour.

When , stricter real-time configuration is applied: timestamp max-age drops from 5 minutes to 5 seconds, and sequence numbers are checked for continuity. Disable for historical backfill or replay scenarios.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.ValidationPipelineConfig.Enabled
member.summary: When , the F# validation stage is activated. Defaults to to preserve backward-compatible behaviour.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Config.ValidationPipelineConfig.UseRealTimeMode
member.summary: When , stricter real-time configuration is applied: timestamp max-age drops from 5 minutes to 5 seconds, and sequence numbers are checked for continuity. Disable for historical backfill or replay scenarios.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.BackfillConfig
member.summary: Configuration for historical backfill operations.
member.param: When true, the collector will run a backfill instead of live collection.
Symbols to backfill; defaults to configured live symbols.
When true and using composite provider, automatically try alternative providers on failure.
Prefer providers that return adjusted prices (for splits/dividends).
Use OpenFIGI to normalize symbols across providers.
Custom provider priority order for fallback (overrides defaults).
Automatically rotate to next provider when approaching rate limits.
Usage threshold (0.0-1.0) at which to start rotating providers.
Check existing archives and skip dates that already have data.
Only fill detected gaps in existing data (vs. full backfill).

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.CanonicalizationConfig
member.summary: Configuration for the deterministic canonicalization pipeline (Phase 2). Controls whether events are enriched with canonical symbols, venues, and condition codes before entering the EventPipeline.
member.param: Master switch for the canonicalization pipeline. Default is false.
Canonicalization mapping version stamped on enriched events. Bumped when mapping tables change.
Optional list of symbols to canonicalize. When empty or null, all symbols are canonicalized. Use this for incremental rollout validation before enabling for the full universe.
When true, both raw and enriched events are persisted for parity validation. When false, only the enriched event is persisted.
Alert threshold for unresolved mapping rate per provider. Default is 0.1%. When exceeded for 5+ minutes, a warning is logged.
Path to condition-codes.json mapping file. Null uses built-in defaults.
Path to venue-mapping.json mapping file. Null uses built-in defaults.

Optional list of symbols to canonicalize. When empty or null, all symbols are canonicalized. Use this for incremental rollout validation before enabling for the full universe.
When true, both raw and enriched events are persisted for parity validation. When false, only the enriched event is persisted.
Alert threshold for unresolved mapping rate per provider. Default is 0.1%. When exceeded for 5+ minutes, a warning is logged.
Path to condition-codes.json mapping file. Null uses built-in defaults.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.IConfigurationProvider
member.summary: Unified configuration provider interface for consistent access to application configuration across all components.
member.remarks:
Addresses scattered configuration patterns by providing: - Single interface for all configuration access - Type-safe configuration retrieval - Change notification support - Validation with metadata - Environment variable override support
Consolidates: - AppConfig direct access - ConfigWatcher file monitoring - ConfigEnvironmentOverride environment handling - Various Options/Config record types (19+ types)

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Config.ValidatedConfig
member.summary: Represents a validated, normalized configuration that has passed through the full configuration pipeline. This is the single output type from all configuration entry points (wizard, auto-config, file load, hot reload).

item.description: Environment variable overrides have been applied
Credentials have been resolved from all sources
Self-healing fixes have been applied (if enabled)

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Monitoring.MigrationDiagnostics
member.summary:
Temporary observability counters for the provider-registry migration. Tracks factory hit counts, reconnect attempts, and resubscription outcomes so that behavioral parity can be verified during migration phases.
Part of Phase 0 — Baseline & Safety Rails (refactor-map Step 0.2).
These counters are intentionally additive: they do not alter runtime behavior and can be removed once the migration is complete (Phase 7).
NOTE: This class lives in the Core project (not Application) so that Infrastructure can reference it without creating a circular dependency. The namespace is kept as Meridian.Application.Monitoring for consistency with other monitoring abstractions in this namespace.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy
member.summary:
Central configuration policy for bounded-channel pipelines. Provides consistent backpressure settings across the application.
Use static presets for common scenarios:
- General-purpose event pipelines (100k capacity, DropOldest)
- Streaming data pipelines (50k capacity, DropOldest)
- Internal message buffering (50k capacity, DropOldest, no metrics)
- Background tasks (100 capacity, Wait/backpressure)
- Log channels (1k capacity, DropOldest)
- Completion notifications (500 capacity, Wait/backpressure)
member.remarks: All channel creation in the application should use this policy to ensure consistent backpressure behavior. Use for direct channel creation or when you need to configure additional options.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Threading.Channels.BoundedChannelFullMode,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Central configuration policy for bounded-channel pipelines. Provides consistent backpressure settings across the application.
Use static presets for common scenarios:
- General-purpose event pipelines (100k capacity, DropOldest)
- Streaming data pipelines (50k capacity, DropOldest)
- Internal message buffering (50k capacity, DropOldest, no metrics)
- Background tasks (100 capacity, Wait/backpressure)
- Log channels (1k capacity, DropOldest)
- Completion notifications (500 capacity, Wait/backpressure)
member.remarks: All channel creation in the application should use this policy to ensure consistent backpressure behavior. Use for direct channel creation or when you need to configure additional options.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.Default
member.summary: Default policy for general-purpose event pipelines. High capacity (100k), drops oldest on overflow, metrics enabled.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.HighThroughput
member.summary: Policy for high-throughput streaming data pipelines (e.g., market data clients). Moderate capacity (50k), drops oldest on overflow, metrics enabled for monitoring.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.MessageBuffer
member.summary: Policy for internal message buffering channels (e.g., StockSharp message buffer). Moderate capacity (50k), drops oldest on overflow, metrics disabled for performance.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.MaintenanceQueue
member.summary: Policy for background task/maintenance queues where no messages should be dropped. Low capacity (100), waits when full (backpressure), metrics disabled.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.Logging
member.summary: Policy for logging channels. Low capacity (1k), drops oldest on overflow, metrics disabled.

member.name: P:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.CompletionQueue
member.summary: Policy for completion notification channels (e.g., backfill request completions). Moderate capacity (500), waits when full (no drops), metrics disabled. Use this for channels where completion notifications must not be lost.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.CreateChannel``1(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a bounded channel with this policy's configuration. This is the preferred method for creating channels to ensure consistent backpressure behavior.
member.typeparam: The type of items in the channel.
member.param: Whether there is a single consumer reading from the channel. Default is true.
Whether there is a single producer writing to the channel. Default is false.

member.name: M:Meridian.Application.Pipeline.EventPipelinePolicy.ToBoundedOptions(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a instance from this policy. Use this when you need to further customize channel options before creation.
member.param: Whether there is a single consumer reading from the channel.
Whether there is a single producer writing to the channel.

member.name: T:Meridian.Application.Serialization.MarketDataJsonContext
member.summary:
High-performance JSON serialization using source generators. Eliminates reflection overhead for faster serialization/deserialization.
Based on: System.Text.Json source generators (.NET 7+) Reference: docs/open-source-references.md - System.Text.Json High-Performance Techniques
USAGE: All JSON serialization in this codebase should use one of the following: - MarketDataJsonContext.HighPerformanceOptions for compact output (storage, wire protocol) - MarketDataJsonContext.PrettyPrintOptions for human-readable output (debugging, config files) - HighPerformanceJson static methods for MarketEvent serialization

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Serialization.MarketDataJsonContext.HighPerformanceOptions
member.summary: Pre-configured options for high-performance serialization. Use for storage, wire protocols, and any performance-critical path. - Compact output (no indentation) - CamelCase property naming - Null values omitted - Case-insensitive property matching on read - Source-generated serializers (no reflection)

member.name: F:Meridian.Application.Serialization.MarketDataJsonContext.PrettyPrintOptions
member.summary: Pre-configured options for pretty-printed output (debugging, config files). - Indented output for readability - CamelCase property naming - Null values omitted - Case-insensitive property matching on read - Source-generated serializers (no reflection)

member.name: T:Meridian.Core.Performance.RawQuoteEvent
member.summary: Zero-allocation struct representation of a Best-Bid/Offer quote event for the hot path. Uses for predictable memory layout in the backing array.
member.remarks: The symbol is stored as a pre-computed integer ID from to avoid string references on the producer thread. Mid-price and spread are intentionally omitted — they are derived fields that the consumer can recompute from bid/ask without extra storage.

member.name: T:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1
member.summary: Lock-free, single-producer/single-consumer ring buffer backed by a pre-allocated array.

remarks.para: No allocation occurs on or calls — the backing array is allocated once in the constructor. This makes the buffer suitable for the zero-allocation hot path that handles high-frequency trade and quote events.
The buffer capacity is rounded up to the nearest power of two so index masking (index & _mask) replaces the modulo operation on every slot access.
Thread-safety contract: exactly one producer thread may call , and exactly one consumer thread may call / . Calling either method from more than one thread simultaneously violates this contract.
Head and tail counters are placed in separate cache-line-padded structs to prevent false sharing between the producer and consumer CPU cores.

member.typeparam: Value type stored in the ring buffer. Must be a struct.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new ring buffer with a capacity rounded up to the next power of two.

member.name: P:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.Capacity
member.summary: Gets the actual buffer capacity (the next power of two ≥ the requested capacity).

member.name: P:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.Count
member.summary: Gets an approximate count of items currently available to read. The value may be stale by the time the caller acts on it.

member.name: P:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.IsEmpty
member.summary: Gets whether the buffer currently has no items to read.

member.name: P:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.IsFull
member.summary: Gets whether the buffer is currently full.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.TryWrite(`0@)
member.summary: Tries to write into the buffer.
member.remarks: Must be called from the producer thread only. This method never allocates and performs no locking.
member.returns: when the item was written; when the buffer is full.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.TryRead(`0@)
member.summary: Tries to read the next item from the buffer.
member.remarks: Must be called from the consumer thread only. This method never allocates and performs no locking.
member.param: Set to the dequeued item on success; set to on failure.
member.returns: when an item was dequeued; when the buffer is empty.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.SpscRingBuffer`1.DrainTo(System.Span{`0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Drains up to items into .
member.remarks: Must be called from the consumer thread only.
member.param: Span to write items into. Capacity must be ≥ .
Maximum items to drain. Defaults to the full destination length.
member.returns: The number of items written to .

member.name: T:Meridian.Core.Performance.SymbolTable
member.summary: Thread-safe symbol intern table that maps market symbol strings to compact integer IDs.
member.remarks:
The hot-path producer stores only the integer ID in and to avoid string references on the critical publish path. The consumer resolves the ID back to a symbol string for serialization via .
IDs start at 1. ID 0 is reserved to represent an unregistered symbol. A single global instance should be shared between the producer and consumer so both sides see the same ID→symbol mapping.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.SymbolTable.GetOrAdd(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the integer ID for , registering it if not already present. This call is allocation-free for symbols that have already been registered.
member.returns: Stable non-zero integer ID for the symbol.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.SymbolTable.TryGetSymbol(System.Int32)
member.summary: Resolves an integer ID back to its symbol string.
member.returns: The symbol string, or if was never registered in this table.

member.name: P:Meridian.Core.Performance.SymbolTable.Count
member.summary: Gets the total number of symbols registered in this table.

member.name: T:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities
member.summary: Low-level threading utilities for high-performance scenarios. Provides thread affinity, priority, and CPU pinning capabilities.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.SetHighPriority
member.summary: Sets the current thread's priority to highest for latency-critical operations.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.SetAboveNormalPriority
member.summary: Sets the current thread's priority to above normal (less aggressive than highest).

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.TrySetThreadAffinity(System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to set thread affinity to a specific CPU core (Windows/Linux).
member.returns: True if affinity was set successfully

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.TrySetProcessAffinity(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Attempts to set process affinity to specific CPU cores. Only supported on Windows and Linux.
member.param: Bitmask of allowed CPUs (e.g., 0x3 = CPUs 0 and 1)

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.TrySetProcessHighPriority
member.summary: Attempts to set the process to high priority class. Only supported on Windows and Linux. Use sparingly - this affects all threads and can starve other processes.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.YieldProcessor
member.summary: Yields the current thread to allow other threads to execute. More efficient than Thread.Sleep(0) for busy-wait scenarios.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.SpinWait(System.Int32)
member.summary: Performs a spin-wait for the specified number of iterations. Useful for very short waits where context switching is too expensive.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.GetCurrentThreadId
member.summary: Gets the current thread's managed thread ID.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.TryStartNoGCRegion(System.Int64)
member.summary: Prevents the GC from relocating the current thread's stack during critical sections. Call EndNoGCRegion when done.

member.name: M:Meridian.Core.Performance.ThreadingUtilities.EndNoGCRegion
member.summary: Ends the no-GC region started by TryStartNoGCRegion.

member.name: T:Meridian.Core.Scheduling.CronExpressionParser
member.summary: Lightweight cron expression parser supporting standard 5-field format. Format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week Examples: "0 2 * * *" - Daily at 2:00 AM "0 3 * * 0" - Every Sunday at 3:00 AM "30 6 * * 1-5" - Weekdays at 6:30 AM "0 0 1 * *" - First day of each month at midnight "*/15 * * * *" - Every 15 minutes
